column3: When I face academic difficulties, I believe I can find a way to overcome them.
column4: I take responsibility for improving my situation when things go wrong in my studies.
column5: A setback in one subject does not affect my confidence in other subjects.
column6: I believe most academic problems are temporary and can be resolved with effort.
column7: Even under pressure, I feel I have control over how I respond to academic challenges.
column8: I reflect on my mistakes and use them to perform better next time.
column9: Academic failures do not define my overall ability as a student.
column10: I remain motivated even when results are not immediately visible.
column11: I believe my actions can positively influence my academic outcomes.
column12: I recover quickly after experiencing disappointment in my studies.
